sections.title: SKILLS 11-13

parts.title: SKILL 11: LISTEN FOR EXPRESSIONS OF AGREEMENT
parts.instruction: In this exercise, listen carefully to each short dialogue and question on the recording, and then choose the best answer to the question. You should pay attention to expressions of agreement.

questions.passage:
(man) I'd like to take a trip down the coast this weekend.
(woman) Me, too.
questions.question: 1. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: The trip would cost too much.

reason.en: This is incorrect because the woman's statement 'Me, too' indicates agreement, not a concern about cost.
reason.id: Ini salah karena pernyataan wanita 'Me, too' menunjukkan persetujuan, bukan kekhawatiran tentang biaya.

options.text: She doesn't think that a trip would be a good idea.

reason.en: This is incorrect because 'Me, too' is an expression of agreement, meaning she wants to do the same thing, not that she thinks it's a bad idea.
reason.id: Ini salah karena 'Me, too' adalah ekspresi persetujuan, yang berarti dia ingin melakukan hal yang sama, bukan bahwa dia berpikir itu ide yang buruk.

options.text: She would like to take two trips rather than one.

reason.en: This is incorrect. The phrase 'Me, too' means she wants to do the same thing as the man, not that she wants to take two trips.
reason.id: Ini salah. Frasa 'Me, too' berarti dia ingin melakukan hal yang sama seperti pria itu, bukan bahwa dia ingin melakukan dua perjalanan.

options.text: She would also like to take a trip.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 11: Listen for Expressions of Agreement, the woman's response 'Me, too' directly indicates that she shares the man's desire to take a trip down the coast.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 11: Listen for Expressions of Agreement, respons wanita 'Me, too' secara langsung menunjukkan bahwa dia memiliki keinginan yang sama dengan pria itu untuk melakukan perjalanan menyusuri pantai.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Skill 11 - Listen for Expressions of Agreement

questions.passage:
(woman) I would like to see Matt elected to the town council next month.
(man) So would I.
questions.question: 2. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He would like to see the elections for town council.

reason.en: The man's statement 'So would I' directly expresses agreement with the woman's desire for Matt to be elected, not a general interest in the elections themselves.
reason.id: Pernyataan pria 'So would I' secara langsung mengungkapkan persetujuan terhadap keinginan wanita agar Matt terpilih, bukan minat umum pada pemilihan itu sendiri.

options.text: He agrees that Matt should be elected.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 11: Listen for Expressions of Agreement, the phrase 'So would I' is a common way to express agreement with a previous statement. The man is agreeing with the woman's wish for Matt to be elected.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 11: Listen for Expressions of Agreement, frasa 'So would I' adalah cara umum untuk mengungkapkan persetujuan dengan pernyataan sebelumnya. Pria tersebut setuju dengan keinginan wanita agar Matt terpilih.

options.text: He thinks the elections should take place next month.

reason.en: While the woman mentions 'next month,' the man's agreement is specifically about Matt being elected, not about the timing of the elections in general.
reason.id: Meskipun wanita menyebutkan 'bulan depan', persetujuan pria tersebut secara khusus adalah tentang Matt yang terpilih, bukan tentang waktu pemilihan secara umum.

options.text: He disagrees with the woman.

reason.en: The phrase 'So would I' is an expression of agreement, directly contradicting the idea that the man disagrees with the woman.
reason.id: Frasa 'So would I' adalah ekspresi persetujuan, yang secara langsung bertentangan dengan gagasan bahwa pria tersebut tidak setuju dengan wanita.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Skill 11 - Listen for Expressions of Agreement

questions.passage:
(man) I'm not sure if I should take begin ning or intermediate French next semester.
(woman) Neither am I.
questions.question: 3. WHAT DOES THE W7OMAN MEAN?

options.text: She is not sure which course she should take.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 7: LISTEN FOR NEGATIVE EXPRESSIONS, the woman uses the phrase 'Neither am I' to agree with the man's statement of uncertainty. This directly means she shares his indecision about which French course to take.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 7: LISTEN FOR NEGATIVE EXPRESSIONS, wanita tersebut menggunakan frasa 'Neither am I' untuk menyetujui pernyataan ketidakpastian pria. Ini secara langsung berarti dia berbagi ketidakputusannya tentang kursus Bahasa Prancis mana yang harus diambil.

options.text: She's not sure if she should take a trip to France.

reason.en: This option introduces the idea of a 'trip to France,' which is not mentioned or implied in the dialogue. The dialogue is about choosing a French course.
reason.id: Opsi ini memperkenalkan gagasan tentang 'perjalanan ke Prancis', yang tidak disebutkan atau tersirat dalam dialog. Dialog tersebut adalah tentang memilih kursus Bahasa Prancis.

options.text: She knows that she is not ready for intermediate French.

reason.en: The woman's statement 'Neither am I' indicates she shares the man's uncertainty. It does not specifically state she is not ready for intermediate French; she is unsure about both options.
reason.id: Pernyataan wanita 'Neither am I' menunjukkan bahwa dia berbagi ketidakpastian pria. Pernyataan itu tidak secara spesifik menyatakan bahwa dia tidak siap untuk Bahasa Prancis tingkat menengah; dia tidak yakin tentang kedua pilihan.

options.text: She wants to take neither beginning nor intermediate French.

reason.en: The phrase 'Neither am I' is an agreement with the man's uncertainty, not a statement that she wants to take neither course. She is undecided, not that she wants to avoid both.
reason.id: Frasa 'Neither am I' adalah persetujuan atas ketidakpastian pria, bukan pernyataan bahwa dia tidak ingin mengambil kedua kursus tersebut. Dia tidak yakin, bukan berarti dia ingin menghindari keduanya.

questions.tested_skill: LISTENING - PART A: SHORT DIALOGUES - Skill 7: LISTEN FOR NEGATIVE EXPRESSIONS

questions.passage:
(man) The food in the cafeteria is not exactly the best food I have ever tasted.
(woman) You can say that again!
questions.question: 4. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: The man should repeat what he said.

reason.en: The woman's statement 'You can say that again!' is an idiom expressing agreement, not a literal request for the man to repeat himself.
reason.id: Pernyataan wanita 'You can say that again!' adalah idiom yang mengekspresikan persetujuan, bukan permintaan harfiah agar pria itu mengulang perkataannya.

options.text: The man said something foolish.

reason.en: The idiom 'You can say that again!' is used to strongly agree with someone, not to call their statement foolish.
reason.id: Idiom 'You can say that again!' digunakan untuk sangat setuju dengan seseorang, bukan untuk menyebut pernyataan mereka bodoh.

options.text: She thinks that the food is the best she has ever tasted.

reason.en: The woman's statement is a strong agreement with the man's negative comment about the food. She does not think it is the best.
reason.id: Pernyataan wanita tersebut adalah persetujuan kuat terhadap komentar negatif pria tentang makanan. Dia tidak berpikir itu yang terbaik.

options.text: She agrees that the food is pretty bad.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 11: Listen for Expressions of Agreement, the phrase 'You can say that again!' is an idiomatic expression used to indicate strong agreement with the previous statement. The man stated the food is 'not exactly the best,' and the woman's response signifies she shares this negative opinion.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 11: Listen for Expressions of Agreement, frasa 'You can say that again!' adalah ekspresi idiomatik yang digunakan untuk menunjukkan persetujuan kuat terhadap pernyataan sebelumnya. Pria itu menyatakan makanan 'tidak persis yang terbaik', dan respons wanita itu menandakan bahwa dia memiliki pendapat negatif yang sama.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Short Dialogues - Skill 11: Listen for Expressions of Agreement

questions.passage:
(woman) This party certainly has been fun!
(man) I'll say!
questions.question: 5. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: This party hasn't been any fun at all.

reason.en: This is the opposite of what the man means. The phrase 'I'll say!' is an expression of strong agreement.
reason.id: Ini adalah kebalikan dari apa yang dimaksud pria itu. Frasa 'I'll say!' adalah ekspresi persetujuan yang kuat.

options.text: He wonders if the woman enjoyed herself.

reason.en: The man is not asking a question; he is agreeing with the woman's statement.
reason.id: Pria itu tidak mengajukan pertanyaan; dia setuju dengan pernyataan wanita itu.

options.text: He wants to know what she said.

reason.en: The man heard the woman clearly and is agreeing with her, not asking for clarification.
reason.id: Pria itu mendengar wanita itu dengan jelas dan setuju dengannya, bukan meminta klarifikasi.

options.text: He's enjoyed himself tremendously.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 11: Listen for Expressions of Agreement, the phrase 'I'll say!' is an idiomatic expression used to strongly agree with a previous statement. The woman said the party has been fun, and the man's response indicates he agrees and has enjoyed it very much.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 11: Listen for Expressions of Agreement, frasa 'I'll say!' adalah ekspresi idiomatik yang digunakan untuk sangat setuju dengan pernyataan sebelumnya. Wanita itu berkata pesta itu menyenangkan, dan respons pria itu menunjukkan bahwa dia setuju dan sangat menikmatinya.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Short Dialogues - Skill 11: Listen for Expressions of Agreement

questions.passage:
(man) I'm not about to condone what she did!
(woman) I'm not either.
questions.question: 6. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She condones what happened.

reason.en: This is incorrect because the woman explicitly states 'I'm not either,' which is the opposite of condoning the action. This option misinterprets the negative agreement.
reason.id: Ini salah karena wanita itu secara eksplisit menyatakan 'Saya juga tidak', yang merupakan kebalikan dari menyetujui tindakan tersebut. Pilihan ini salah menafsirkan persetujuan negatif.

options.text: She does not like what the man said.

reason.en: This is incorrect. The dialogue is about the woman's actions, not about what the man said. The woman's statement 'I'm not either' refers to her agreement with the man's disapproval of her actions.
reason.id: Ini salah. Dialog tersebut adalah tentang tindakan wanita itu, bukan tentang apa yang dikatakan pria itu. Pernyataan wanita 'Saya juga tidak' mengacu pada persetujuannya dengan ketidaksetujuan pria terhadap tindakannya.

options.text: She agrees with the man about what happened.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 11: Listen for Expressions of Agreement, the woman's statement 'I'm not either' is a form of agreement with the man's negative sentiment. The man says 'I'm not about to condone what she did!' and the woman agrees by saying she also does not condone it.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 11: Listen for Expressions of Agreement, pernyataan wanita 'Saya juga tidak' adalah bentuk persetujuan dengan sentimen negatif pria. Pria itu berkata 'Saya tidak akan memaafkan apa yang dia lakukan!' dan wanita itu setuju dengan mengatakan bahwa dia juga tidak memaafkannya.

options.text: She says that she did not do it.

reason.en: This is incorrect. The dialogue does not discuss whether the woman did the action or not. The focus is on the man's and woman's feelings about the action itself.
reason.id: Ini salah. Dialog tersebut tidak membahas apakah wanita itu melakukan tindakan tersebut atau tidak. Fokusnya adalah pada perasaan pria dan wanita tentang tindakan itu sendiri.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Short Dialogues - Skill 11: Listen for Expressions of Agreement

questions.passage:
(woman) Those people upstairs always have such loud parties.
(man) Don't they!
questions.question: 7. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He thinks the parties aren't loud.

reason.en: The man's response 'Don't they!' is an expression of agreement, meaning he agrees with the woman's statement that the parties are loud. This option incorrectly states he thinks the parties aren't loud.
reason.id: Respons pria 'Don't they!' adalah ekspresi persetujuan, yang berarti dia setuju dengan pernyataan wanita bahwa pesta-pesta itu keras. Pilihan ini secara keliru menyatakan bahwa dia berpikir pesta-pesta itu tidak keras.

options.text: He says that the neighbors don't have many parties.

reason.en: The dialogue focuses on the loudness of the parties, not the frequency. The man's agreement ('Don't they!') directly addresses the loudness, not the number of parties.
reason.id: Dialog berfokus pada kekerasan pesta, bukan frekuensinya. Persetujuan pria ('Don't they!') secara langsung membahas kekerasan, bukan jumlah pesta.

options.text: He agrees that the upstairs neighbors are noisy.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 11: LISTEN FOR EXPRESSIONS OF AGREEMENT, the phrase 'Don't they!' is a common way to express agreement with a negative statement. The woman's statement implies the parties are loud, and the man's response confirms he agrees with this observation.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 11: LISTEN FOR EXPRESSIONS OF AGREEMENT, frasa 'Don't they!' adalah cara umum untuk menyatakan persetujuan dengan pernyataan negatif. Pernyataan wanita menyiratkan bahwa pesta-pesta itu keras, dan respons pria mengonfirmasi bahwa dia setuju dengan pengamatan ini.

options.text: The loud parties don't bother him.

reason.en: The man's agreement ('Don't they!') indicates he acknowledges the loudness of the parties. This option suggests indifference, which is not supported by his direct agreement.
reason.id: Persetujuan pria ('Don't they!') menunjukkan bahwa dia mengakui kekerasan pesta. Pilihan ini menyarankan ketidakpedulian, yang tidak didukung oleh persetujuannya yang langsung.

questions.tested_skill: LISTENING - PART A: SHORT DIALOGUES - Skill 11: LISTEN FOR EXPRESSIONS OF AGREEMENT

questions.passage:
(man) This meal is really delicious!
(woman) Isn't it!
questions.question: 8. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She doesn't like this meal too much.

reason.en: This is incorrect because the woman's response 'Isn't it!' is a tag question used to express agreement, not disagreement.
reason.id: Ini salah karena respons wanita 'Isn't it!' adalah pertanyaan tag yang digunakan untuk menyatakan persetujuan, bukan ketidaksetujuan.

options.text: This food tastes wonderful to her.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 11: Listen for Expressions of Agreement, the woman's response 'Isn't it!' is a tag question that strongly agrees with the man's statement that the meal is delicious. It implies the food tastes wonderful to her.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 11: Listen for Expressions of Agreement, respons wanita 'Isn't it!' adalah pertanyaan tag yang sangat setuju dengan pernyataan pria bahwa makanannya lezat. Ini menyiratkan bahwa makanannya terasa luar biasa baginya.

options.text: She's not sure if she likes it.

reason.en: This is incorrect. The phrase 'Isn't it!' is a strong expression of agreement, not uncertainty.
reason.id: Ini salah. Frasa 'Isn't it!' adalah ekspresi persetujuan yang kuat, bukan ketidakpastian.

options.text: She can't stand this meal.

reason.en: This is incorrect. The woman's response indicates agreement and enjoyment, the opposite of 'can't stand'.
reason.id: Ini salah. Respons wanita menunjukkan persetujuan dan kenikmatan, kebalikan dari 'tidak tahan'.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Skill 11 - Listen for Expressions of Agreement

questions.passage:
(man) I can't imagine what he was thinking about when he bought that car.
(woman) Neither can I!
questions.question: 9. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She agrees that getting the car was not a good idea.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 7: LISTEN FOR NEGATIVE EXPRESSIONS, the woman uses 'Neither can I!' which is an agreement with the man's negative statement. 'Neither can I' means 'I also cannot imagine what he was thinking,' implying she agrees that buying the car was not a good idea.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 7: LISTEN FOR NEGATIVE EXPRESSIONS, wanita tersebut menggunakan 'Neither can I!' yang merupakan persetujuan terhadap pernyataan negatif pria. 'Neither can I' berarti 'Saya juga tidak bisa membayangkan apa yang dia pikirkan,' yang menyiratkan bahwa dia setuju bahwa membeli mobil itu bukanlah ide yang bagus.

options.text: She imagines that she would like to have a similar car.

reason.en: This option is incorrect. The woman's statement 'Neither can I!' is a negative agreement, meaning she cannot imagine it either, not that she wants a similar car.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah. Pernyataan wanita 'Neither can I!' adalah persetujuan negatif, yang berarti dia juga tidak bisa membayangkannya, bukan berarti dia menginginkan mobil yang serupa.

options.text: She thinks that the man is mistaken about the car.

reason.en: This option is incorrect. The woman is agreeing with the man's sentiment about the car purchase, not suggesting the man is mistaken.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah. Wanita tersebut setuju dengan perasaan pria mengenai pembelian mobil, bukan menyarankan bahwa pria itu salah.

options.text: She thinks the man has no imagination.

reason.en: This option is incorrect. The man's statement is about his inability to imagine the car purchase, not about his general imagination. The woman's response is an agreement with his specific point.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah. Pernyataan pria adalah tentang ketidakmampuannya membayangkan pembelian mobil, bukan tentang imajinasinya secara umum. Tanggapan wanita adalah persetujuan terhadap poin spesifiknya.

questions.tested_skill: LISTENING - PART A: SHORT DIALOGUES - Skill 7: LISTEN FOR NEGATIVE EXPRESSIONS

questions.passage:
(woman) This should be a two-semester course.
(man) One semester is just not enough time to learn all the material.
questions.question: 10. You can say that again! WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He would like the woman to repeat what she said.

reason.en: The man's statement 'You can say that again!' is an idiom expressing strong agreement, not a literal request for repetition.
reason.id: Pernyataan pria 'You can say that again!' adalah idiom yang mengekspresikan persetujuan kuat, bukan permintaan harfiah untuk mengulang.

options.text: He thinks that one semester is enough time for the course.

reason.en: This option directly contradicts the man's statement. He says 'One semester is just not enough time,' meaning he disagrees with the idea that one semester is sufficient.
reason.id: Opsi ini secara langsung bertentangan dengan pernyataan pria. Dia berkata 'Satu semester saja tidak cukup waktu,' yang berarti dia tidak setuju dengan gagasan bahwa satu semester sudah cukup.

options.text: He also thinks that the course should be extended.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 11: Listen for Expressions of Agreement, the idiom 'You can say that again!' means the speaker strongly agrees with the previous statement. The woman stated that the course should be two semesters, and the man's response indicates he agrees and believes one semester is insufficient.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 11: Listen for Expressions of Agreement, idiom 'You can say that again!' berarti pembicara sangat setuju dengan pernyataan sebelumnya. Wanita itu menyatakan bahwa mata kuliah tersebut seharusnya dua semester, dan respons pria itu menunjukkan bahwa dia setuju dan percaya bahwa satu semester tidak cukup.

options.text: He would like to take the course two semesters from now.

reason.en: The dialogue is about the duration of a course, not about when the man wants to take it. This option introduces an irrelevant time frame.
reason.id: Dialog ini tentang durasi suatu mata kuliah, bukan tentang kapan pria itu ingin mengambilnya. Opsi ini memperkenalkan kerangka waktu yang tidak relevan.

parts.title: SKILL 12: LISTEN FOR EXPRESSIONS OF UNCERTAINTY AND SUGGESTION
parts.instruction: TOEFL EXERCISE 12: In this exercise, listen carefully to each short dialogue and question on the recording, and then choose the best answer to the question. You should be particularly careful of expressions of uncertainty and suggestion.

questions.passage:
(woman) Are we supposed to read all ten chap ters before the exam?
(man) As far as I can tell, we are.
questions.question: 1. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He's sure about which chapters they are to read.

reason.en: The man is not sure about the exact number of chapters, only that they are supposed to read them before the exam. The phrase 'As far as I can tell' indicates uncertainty.
reason.id: Pria itu tidak yakin tentang jumlah bab yang tepat, hanya bahwa mereka seharusnya membacanya sebelum ujian. Frasa 'Sejauh yang saya tahu' menunjukkan ketidakpastian.

options.text: He thinks he knows what the assignment is.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, the man's statement 'As far as I can tell, we are' is a paraphrase of 'He thinks he knows what the assignment is.' He is expressing his understanding of the assignment, even with a slight reservation indicated by 'As far as I can tell.'
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 2: Pilih Jawaban dengan Sinonim, pernyataan pria 'Sejauh yang saya tahu, kita harus' adalah parafrase dari 'Dia pikir dia tahu apa tugasnya.' Dia mengungkapkan pemahamannya tentang tugas tersebut, bahkan dengan sedikit keraguan yang ditunjukkan oleh 'Sejauh yang saya tahu.'

options.text: He has to tell her how far she should go.

reason.en: The man is not giving instructions on how far the woman should read; he is discussing the reading assignment for the exam.
reason.id: Pria itu tidak memberikan instruksi tentang seberapa jauh wanita itu harus membaca; dia sedang mendiskusikan tugas membaca untuk ujian.

options.text: The professor told them to read the chapters after the exam.

reason.en: The dialogue clearly states the reading is 'before the exam,' not after. This option directly contradicts the information given.
reason.id: Dialog tersebut dengan jelas menyatakan bahwa membaca adalah 'sebelum ujian', bukan sesudah. Pilihan ini secara langsung bertentangan dengan informasi yang diberikan.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Skill 2 - Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
(man) You're out of apple pie!
(woman) Sorry. Why not try the cherry pie?
questions.question: 2. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN SUG GEST?

options.text: The man should take the pie out.

reason.en: The woman suggests trying the cherry pie because the apple pie is unavailable. This option misinterprets the suggestion as an instruction to take the pie out, which is not implied.
reason.id: Wanita itu menyarankan untuk mencoba pai ceri karena pai apel tidak tersedia. Pilihan ini salah menafsirkan saran sebagai instruksi untuk mengeluarkan pai, yang tidak tersirat.

options.text: The man should try something else.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, the woman's suggestion 'Why not try the cherry pie?' is a polite way of suggesting an alternative since the apple pie is out. This option accurately captures the meaning of trying something else.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, saran wanita 'Why not try the cherry pie?' adalah cara sopan untuk menyarankan alternatif karena pai apel habis. Pilihan ini secara akurat menangkap makna mencoba sesuatu yang lain.

options.text: The man shouldn't try cherry pie.

reason.en: This option directly contradicts the woman's suggestion. She offers the cherry pie as an alternative, implying it is a good option to try.
reason.id: Pilihan ini secara langsung bertentangan dengan saran wanita tersebut. Dia menawarkan pai ceri sebagai alternatif, menyiratkan bahwa itu adalah pilihan yang baik untuk dicoba.

options.text: The man should feel sorry.

reason.en: The woman says 'Sorry' because the apple pie is out, not as a suggestion for the man to feel sorry. This option misinterprets the function of the word 'sorry' in this context.
reason.id: Wanita itu mengatakan 'Sorry' karena pai apel habis, bukan sebagai saran agar pria itu merasa bersalah. Pilihan ini salah menafsirkan fungsi kata 'sorry' dalam konteks ini.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Short Dialogues - Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
(woman) Do you know when the movie starts?
(man) It starts at 8:00, doesn't it?
questions.question: 3. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He knows the movie starts at 8:00.

reason.en: The man uses a tag question ('doesn't it?') which indicates uncertainty, not certainty. He is not stating a fact but seeking confirmation.
reason.id: Pria itu menggunakan pertanyaan tag ('doesn't it?') yang menunjukkan ketidakpastian, bukan kepastian. Dia tidak menyatakan fakta tetapi mencari konfirmasi.

options.text: He is not quite sure when the movie begins.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 12: Listen for Expressions of Uncertainty and Suggestion, the man's use of the tag question 'doesn't it?' clearly signals that he is not sure about the movie's start time and is asking for confirmation.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 12: Listen for Expressions of Uncertainty and Suggestion, penggunaan pertanyaan tag oleh pria tersebut 'doesn't it?' dengan jelas menandakan bahwa dia tidak yakin tentang waktu mulai film dan sedang meminta konfirmasi.

options.text: He thinks the start of the movie has been changed.

reason.en: There is no information in the dialogue to suggest that the start time has been changed. The man is asking about the current start time.
reason.id: Tidak ada informasi dalam dialog yang menunjukkan bahwa waktu mulai telah diubah. Pria itu bertanya tentang waktu mulai saat ini.

options.text: He will start the movie himself at 8:00.

reason.en: The dialogue is about the movie's start time, not about the man starting the movie himself. This option misinterprets the meaning of 'starts'.
reason.id: Dialog tersebut adalah tentang waktu mulai film, bukan tentang pria itu yang memulai film sendiri. Opsi ini salah menafsirkan arti 'starts'.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Short Dialogues (Skill 12: Listen for Expressions of Uncertainty and Suggestion)

questions.passage:
(woman) We can't leave now. We have to do the dishes.
(man) Let's leave the dishes until later.

The woman's response 'Then, you will take the test this afternoon' suggests she is inferring that he *will* take it. The question asks what she *assumed*. If she assumed he *would not* take the test, her statement would be a direct contradiction or a question, not a conclusion that he *will* take it. However, the prompt is tricky. The woman's statement is a direct inference that he *will* take the test. The question asks what she *assumed*. The most logical assumption she made, given the context, is that he *would not* be taking it if he were still studying *now*. This is a common TOEFL trap where the answer is the opposite of what seems implied by the direct statement, but it's the underlying assumption that is being tested.

Asumsi paling logis yang dia buat, mengingat konteksnya, adalah bahwa dia *tidak akan* mengikutinya jika dia masih belajar *sekarang*. Ini adalah jebakan TOEFL umum di mana jawabannya adalah kebalikan dari apa yang tampak tersirat oleh pernyataan langsung, tetapi itu adalah asumsi mendasar yang sedang diuji.

sections.instruction: Expressions of agreement are common in the short dialogues, so you should become familiar with them. The following example shows agreement with a positive statement.

questions.passage:
(man) The phone bill is certainly high this month!
(woman) I'll say!
questions.question: 1. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She plans to talk a lot this month.

reason.en: This is incorrect. The woman's statement 'I'll say!' is an idiom of agreement, not a statement about her future phone usage.
reason.id: Ini salah. Pernyataan wanita 'I'll say!' adalah idiom persetujuan, bukan pernyataan tentang penggunaan teleponnya di masa depan.

options.text: She has a lot to say about the phone bill.

reason.en: This is incorrect. While she has something to say about the bill, 'I'll say!' specifically means she agrees with the man's observation that the bill is high. It's not about her having many opinions in general.
reason.id: Ini salah. Meskipun dia memiliki sesuatu untuk dikatakan tentang tagihan, 'I'll say!' secara khusus berarti dia setuju dengan pengamatan pria bahwa tagihan itu tinggi. Ini bukan tentang dia memiliki banyak pendapat secara umum.

options.text: The bill is high because she has a lot to say.

reason.en: This is incorrect. The idiom 'I'll say!' expresses agreement with a previous statement. It does not imply that her talking caused the high bill.
reason.id: Ini salah. Idiom 'I'll say!' mengekspresikan persetujuan dengan pernyataan sebelumnya. Ini tidak menyiratkan bahwa perkataannya menyebabkan tagihan yang tinggi.

options.text: She agrees with the man.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 11: Listen for Expressions of Agreement, the woman's response 'I'll say!' is a common idiomatic expression used to strongly agree with what the previous speaker has just said. She is agreeing that the phone bill is indeed high.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 11: Listen for Expressions of Agreement, respons wanita 'I'll say!' adalah ekspresi idiomatik umum yang digunakan untuk sangat setuju dengan apa yang baru saja dikatakan oleh pembicara sebelumnya. Dia setuju bahwa tagihan telepon memang tinggi.

(man) That exam couldn't have been more difficult!
(woman) I'll say!
questions.question: 10.  WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She would like the man to repeat what he said.

reason.en: The woman's response 'I'll say!' is an expression of agreement, not a request for repetition.
reason.id: Respons wanita 'I'll say!' adalah ekspresi persetujuan, bukan permintaan untuk mengulang.

options.text: She thinks the exam could have been a little more difficult.

reason.en: The phrase 'I'll say!' is used to strongly agree with a previous statement. The woman is agreeing that the exam was difficult, not suggesting it could be more difficult.
reason.id: Frasa 'I'll say!' digunakan untuk sangat setuju dengan pernyataan sebelumnya. Wanita itu setuju bahwa ujian itu sulit, bukan menyarankan agar ujian itu bisa lebih sulit.

options.text: She shares the same opinion of the exam as the man.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 11: Listen for Expressions of Agreement, the woman's response 'I'll say!' is an idiomatic way of strongly agreeing with the man's statement that the exam was very difficult. It means 'I agree with you completely.'
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 11: Listen for Expressions of Agreement, respons wanita 'I'll say!' adalah cara idiomatik untuk sangat setuju dengan pernyataan pria bahwa ujian itu sangat sulit. Ini berarti 'Saya sangat setuju dengan Anda.'

options.text: She believes that the exam was easy.

reason.en: The man states the exam 'couldn't have been more difficult,' indicating extreme difficulty. The woman's agreement ('I'll say!') confirms this sentiment, making this option the opposite of what is meant.
